usc-bachelor-of-accounting.overview: USC Accounting (BS) is a first-year undergraduate route. Applicants apply through the Common Application and should check USC Leventhal for any major-specific instructions.

eligibility.intro: Applicants need a completed or pending equivalent of a U.S. high-school diploma before enrollment.
items: Submit secondary-school records equivalent to U.S. grades 9–12.
Send external examination or predicted results where applicable.
International applicants must show approved English proficiency unless USC's policy permits another route.
SAT or ACT scores are optional for USC's 2026–27 first-year cycle.

applicationSteps: Complete the USC first-year Common Application.
Select the Accounting major and pay the application fee or request a waiver.
Arrange direct submission of official academic records and examination results.
Submit approved English-proficiency scores within USC's stated window.
Upload passport and first-year financial-support evidence in the Applicant Portal.
Track the application and complete USC follow-up requests.

academic: Official secondary-school transcripts for all years attended.
External examination or predicted results where applicable.
Certified English translations for non-English records.
application: Common Application and USC-specific responses.
Current passport copy.
Financial Statement of Personal or Family Support with proof of funds.

usc-bachelor-of-accounting.deadlinesNote: USC uses published Early Action and Regular Decision dates; check the current first-year calendar for the intended entry year. Final records follow USC's post-admission schedule.
visaConsiderations: After admission, commitment and financial review, eligible students request an initial I-20 through Trojan International before applying for an F-1 visa.

usc-mba.overview: USC Marshall's Full-Time MBA is a fall-entry, in-person graduate programme. This block applies to the Full-Time MBA route; Marshall publishes separate requirements for other formats.

eligibility.intro: Applicants need a bachelor's degree or equivalent; Marshall welcomes applicants from all academic backgrounds.
items: Hold a bachelor's degree or equivalent.
Submit a professional résumé.
Provide GMAT or GRE scores, or request Marshall's published waiver.
International applicants must meet USC English-proficiency requirements unless exempt.

applicationSteps: Review Full-Time MBA criteria and the current round calendar.
Complete the Marshall application and pay the $200 fee.
Upload the required essay, résumé and transcript scans.
Submit GMAT/GRE scores or request a waiver.
Complete the video assessment and follow-up materials.
Track the application and respond by the stated date.

academic: Official transcripts from every college or university attended.
Country-specific academic records or translations where required.
GMAT or GRE report unless Marshall approves a waiver.
application: Professional résumé and one written essay.
Video assessment and completed Marshall application.
TOEFL or IELTS report unless exempt.

usc-mba.deadlinesNote: For Fall 2026, Marshall listed rounds on October 15, January 15 and April 15. Verify the next cycle; international applicants are encouraged to use earlier rounds for visa timing.
visaConsiderations: After admission, international students complete USC financial documentation and I-20 or DS-2019 processing; the Full-Time MBA is an in-person route.

usc-ms-business-analytics.overview: USC Marshall's MSBA is a full-time, on-campus STEM programme completed over at least three semesters. Admission requires a credited undergraduate statistics course.

eligibility.intro: Applicants need a bachelor's degree or equivalent and a credited statistics course covering the stated topics.
items: Hold a bachelor's degree or equivalent.
Complete college-level statistics covering probability, distributions, inference and introductory regression.
Internship or work experience is preferred but not required.
International applicants need TOEFL or IELTS unless an approved waiver applies.
GMAT and GRE scores are not required.

applicationSteps: Review MSBA criteria and choose the current round.
Complete the Marshall application and pay the $175 fee.
Upload official transcripts, résumé and statistics-course evidence.
Submit English scores if required.
Complete the KIRA interview if invited.
Submit passport and financial documents after admission if requesting an I-20.

academic: Official transcripts from all colleges and universities attended.
Evidence of a statistics course with the required topics.
English-proficiency report unless exempt.
application: Marshall MSBA application and fee.
Professional résumé.
KIRA interview response when invited.

usc-ms-business-analytics.deadlinesNote: The published cycle listed rounds on November 1, January 15 and April 15. Verify the next cycle; Marshall warns that international Round 3 visa timing cannot be guaranteed.
visaConsiderations: The MSBA is full-time and on campus; admitted international students still need USC financial documentation and an I-20 before F-1 visa application.

usc-ms-finance.overview: USC Marshall's MS Finance is a specialized graduate route with a fall start. The current admissions page requires a degree, transcripts, résumé, test scores and a KIRA interview when invited.

eligibility.intro: Applicants need a bachelor's degree or equivalent and the quantitative preparation expected by Marshall.
items: Hold a bachelor's degree or equivalent.
Submit official transcripts from all institutions attended.
Provide official GMAT or GRE scores; waiver requests are not accepted on the current page.
International applicants need TOEFL or IELTS unless USC's policy grants an exemption.

applicationSteps: Review MSF criteria and the current round calendar.
Complete the USC Marshall application and pay the $175 fee.
Upload transcripts and professional résumé.
Send official GMAT or GRE scores.
Complete the KIRA interview if invited.
Track the file until all materials are received by the deadline.

academic: Official transcripts from all colleges and universities attended.
Official GMAT or GRE score report.
English-proficiency report unless exempt.
application: Marshall MSF application and fee.
Professional résumé.
KIRA interview response when invited.

usc-ms-finance.deadlinesNote: For the published cycle, Marshall listed November 1, January 15 and April 15 rounds. Check the live MSF page for the next cycle and apply earlier if visa processing is needed.
visaConsiderations: International admits complete USC financial documentation and I-20 processing after admission; the MSF is a fall-start route.

usc-ms-computer-science.overview: USC Viterbi's MS in Computer Science requires 32 units and offers a thesis option. The programme is listed as eligible for the OPT STEM extension; department prerequisites must be checked before applying.

eligibility.intro: Applicants need a bachelor's degree or must be completing one, normally in computer science or a related field.
items: Hold or be completing a bachelor's degree.
Meet Viterbi's computer-science or related-field preparation; non-CS applicants must review MSCS eligibility criteria.
Submit English-proficiency evidence if USC requires it.
Check the department's current prerequisite and test policy.

applicationSteps: Review the MSCS programme and eligibility criteria.
Complete the USC Graduate Application by the chosen semester deadline.
Upload transcripts and programme materials.
Submit official English scores and any required test scores.
Track the USC portal and complete department follow-up items.
After admission, complete financial and immigration documentation if needed.

academic: Official transcripts from all post-secondary institutions.
Evidence of bachelor's completion or expected completion.
English-proficiency score report where required.
application: USC Graduate Application and fee.
Statement or programme-specific responses as requested.
CV, recommendations or other Viterbi materials if requested.

usc-ms-computer-science.deadlinesNote: Viterbi lists September 15 for Spring, December 15 for Fall scholarship consideration and January 15 as the Fall final deadline. Confirm the cycle and department page before applying.
visaConsiderations: The on-campus MSCS route can support student-visa planning after admission, but USC requires financial documentation before I-20 issuance; online DEN@Viterbi has different visa implications.

usc-ms-applied-data-science.overview: USC's Applied Data Science route combines data management, analytics and machine learning in an interdisciplinary format. Applicants must confirm the current residential or online route before relying on visa or budget assumptions.

eligibility.intro: Applicants need a bachelor's degree or equivalent and the preparation stated by the selected USC data-science route.
items: Hold or be completing a bachelor's degree or equivalent.
Review the current programming, statistics and quantitative preparation for the selected route.
Meet USC English-proficiency requirements if applicable.
Confirm whether the chosen format is residential and eligible for international enrollment.

applicationSteps: Select the current Applied Data Science route and format.
Complete the USC Graduate Application and pay the fee.
Upload official transcripts and required academic records.
Submit English scores and programme materials if required.
Track the USC and department portals for follow-up requests.
After admission, submit financial documents and passport information if requesting an I-20.

academic: Official transcripts from every post-secondary institution.
Evidence of degree completion or expected completion.
English-proficiency report where required.
application: USC Graduate Application and programme responses.
Résumé or CV if requested by the selected route.
Statement, recommendations or project evidence if requested.

usc-ms-applied-data-science.deadlinesNote: USC data-science admissions dates depend on the selected degree and delivery format; no single deadline should be inferred from the university page. Confirm the current programme admissions page.
visaConsiderations: Online-only study does not provide the basis for a U.S. student visa; international applicants must confirm a residential, visa-eligible route before applying.

usc-mph.overview: USC Keck's on-campus MPH requires a bachelor's degree, quantitative preparation, recommendations, a statement of purpose and a résumé. The on-campus application uses SOPHAS; the online route has different requirements.

eligibility.intro: Applicants need a bachelor's degree and the graduate-school preparation required by Keck.
items: Hold a bachelor's degree with at least a 3.0 cumulative GPA.
Complete at least one college-level quantitative course.
Prepare three recent recommendation letters, including at least one academic letter.
Submit a statement of purpose addressing USC fit and career goals.
The GRE is not required for the on-campus MPH.

applicationSteps: Choose the on-campus or online MPH route.
Create a SOPHAS account for the on-campus programme.
Complete the application and pay the application fee.
Submit official transcripts, recommendations, statement and résumé.
Review international requirements and English evidence.
Track SOPHAS and USC requests until the file is complete.

academic: Official transcripts from every accredited college or university.
Evidence of bachelor's degree and 3.0 GPA.
Record of a college-level quantitative course.
application: Statement of Purpose of approximately 2–3 pages.
Curriculum vitae or résumé.
Three recent letters of recommendation, including one academic.

usc-mph.deadlinesNote: Keck lists on-campus Fall priority December 1 and final April 1 deadlines, with Spring October 1 and Summer March 1 dates. Confirm the current cycle; online MPH dates differ.
visaConsiderations: International on-campus MPH students should use the USC financial-documentation and I-20 process after admission; the online MPH is not a basis for an F-1 campus visa.

usc-llm.overview: USC Gould's on-campus one-year LLM is intended for applicants with a first law degree. USC offers fall and spring starts, and international visa deadlines are earlier than the general final deadline.

eligibility.intro: Applicants generally need an LLB, JD or equivalent first law degree before the LLM begins.
items: Hold a first law degree such as an LLB, JD or equivalent.
Internationally issued degrees require transcript authentication and evaluation through LSAC.
Submit TOEFL or IELTS unless USC grants an approved waiver.
Work experience and LSAT/GRE scores are not required for Gould graduate degrees.

applicationSteps: Choose the one-year on-campus LLM start and review its deadline.
Create the LSAC LLM account and USC application.
Arrange transcript authentication and evaluation through LSAC.
Submit the application, fee and personal materials.
Send English scores or an eligible waiver request.
Complete financial and passport documentation after admission if requesting an I-20.

academic: First law degree transcript and degree evidence.
LSAC-authenticated and evaluated transcripts for foreign-issued degrees.
TOEFL or IELTS score report unless waived.
application: USC Gould application and personal statement.
Résumé and any required recommendations or supplemental responses.
Application fee, subject to Gould's priority-deadline waiver policy.

usc-llm.deadlinesNote: For Spring 2027, Gould listed a September 1 priority and November 1 final deadline; international applicants needing an initial I-20 had an October 1 documentation deadline. For Fall 2027, the listed final international deadline was May 1.
visaConsiderations: The on-campus LLM can support F-1 planning after admission; Gould distinguishes it from the online LLM, which cannot issue an I-20.

usc-juris-doctor.overview: USC Gould's JD is a full-time, in-person professional law degree. Applicants need a bachelor's degree before enrollment and apply through LSAC with a CAS report and LSAT or GRE pathway.

eligibility.intro: Applicants must plan to receive a bachelor's degree before enrolling and be able to attend full time in person.
items: Complete a bachelor's degree before JD enrollment.
Register for LSAC's Credential Assembly Service.
Submit an LSAT score or use the permitted GRE route.
Prepare personal statement, résumé and recommendation letters.
International applicants must check USC Gould's foreign-educated and English requirements.

applicationSteps: Create an LSAC account and register for CAS.
Complete the USC Gould electronic JD application.
Send transcripts, test scores and recommendations through CAS.
Upload the personal statement, résumé and required disclosures.
Pay the application fee or request an eligible waiver.
Track the file through LSAC and Gould until complete.

academic: Official post-secondary transcripts sent to LSAC.
CAS report containing transcripts, test scores and letters.
Bachelor's degree confirmation before enrollment.
application: Personal statement and résumé.
LSAT or GRE score report and LSAT Writing where applicable.
Letters of recommendation and qualifications-for-bar disclosure.

usc-juris-doctor.deadlinesNote: For Fall 2027, Gould listed November 15 Early Decision, February 1 priority and April 1 regular deadlines. Verify the next cycle and test dates; late completion is disadvantaged because review is rolling.
visaConsiderations: The JD is full time and in person; admitted international students must complete USC financial documentation and immigration processing, then verify any home-country bar or licensing implications separately.

usc-master-of-architecture.overview: USC Architecture offers graduate architecture tracks whose duration and prerequisites differ by prior education. Applicants must select the correct M.Arch route and submit a portfolio with the graduate application.

eligibility.intro: Eligibility depends on whether the applicant has pre-professional or professional architecture education.
items: Review the selected M.Arch track and its prior-degree requirements.
Provide a complete record of previous college or university work.
Prepare the required design portfolio through USC's stated submission route.
Meet USC English-proficiency and international transcript requirements where applicable.

applicationSteps: Select the appropriate USC Architecture graduate track.
Complete USC's graduate application by the programme deadline.
Upload official transcripts and any required translations.
Submit the design portfolio through the USC architecture platform.
Add statement, résumé, recommendations or supplemental items requested by the track.
Track USC and Architecture communications for interview or follow-up requests.

academic: Official transcripts from all colleges and universities attended.
Evidence of the prior architecture degree required by the selected track.
English-proficiency report where required.
application: Design portfolio.
Statement of intent and résumé where required.
Recommendations or other track-specific supplemental materials.

usc-master-of-architecture.deadlinesNote: USC Architecture's published guidance has used February 1 as the priority deadline for admission and financial aid; verify the current cycle and exact track deadline before applying.
visaConsiderations: International students should confirm that the selected full-time studio route is visa-eligible and complete USC financial documentation before requesting an I-20.

usc-mfa-cinematic-arts.overview: USC Cinematic Arts MFA admissions are division-specific. Applicants submit the USC Graduate Application plus a linked SlideRoom application containing creative materials, recommendations and English evidence where required.

eligibility.intro: Applicants need a bachelor's degree by the start of the intended first term and the creative preparation required by the selected MFA division.
items: Hold or complete a bachelor's degree from a regionally accredited institution.
Choose the exact MFA division because portfolio and writing requirements vary.
Submit official transcripts from all post-secondary institutions.
International applicants need the stated TOEFL, IELTS, PTE or Cambridge score unless exempt.
Provide financial documentation as an international applicant.

applicationSteps: Choose the Cinematic Arts MFA division and fall or eligible spring route.
Complete the USC Graduate Application.
Open the linked SlideRoom application from the application portal.
Upload division-specific portfolio, writing, résumé and personal materials.
Request recommendations through SlideRoom by the deadline.
Track both USC and SlideRoom portals for completeness.

academic: Official transcripts from all colleges and universities attended.
Bachelor's degree evidence or expected conferral.
English-proficiency report within the stated validity period.
application: Division-specific creative portfolio or writing samples.
Résumé or CV and personal statement.
Letters of recommendation through SlideRoom.

usc-mfa-cinematic-arts.deadlinesNote: For Fall graduate admission, USC Cinematic Arts lists November 15 for Animation, Film & Television Production, Interactive Media, Peter Stark Producing and Writing for Screen & Television MFA routes; verify the exact division because Cinema & Media Studies uses December 1.
visaConsiderations: International applicants submit financial documentation before USC can issue an I-20 or DS-2019; creative-production MFA formats are not interchangeable with online study for visa purposes.

usc-phd-computer-science.overview: USC Viterbi doctoral admission is research-led and department-specific. Applicants with a four-year bachelor's degree or equivalent in engineering, mathematics or hard science may be eligible; faculty fit and funding must be checked directly.

eligibility.intro: Applicants need a four-year bachelor's degree or equivalent and preparation aligned with the intended research area.
items: Hold or be completing a four-year bachelor's degree or equivalent.
Show preparation in engineering, mathematics, computer science or a related hard science.
Identify research interests and review USC Computer Science faculty fit.
Meet English-proficiency and any department-specific test requirements.
Do not assume funding is guaranteed; confirm the department's current terms.

applicationSteps: Review Viterbi doctoral fields and the Computer Science department requirements.
Complete the USC Graduate Application by the doctoral deadline.
Upload transcripts and degree-related documents.
Submit recommendations, CV, statement and other required materials.
Send English scores and any required tests.
Track the application even if recommendations or scores arrive after submission.

academic: Official transcripts and degree-related documents.
Evidence of four-year degree equivalency.
English-proficiency report where required.
application: Research statement or statement of purpose.
CV and letters of recommendation.
Programme-specific supplemental materials and test reports if requested.

usc-phd-computer-science.deadlinesNote: Viterbi lists December 15, 2026 at 11:59 p.m. Pacific Time for Fall 2027 doctoral applications; the Fall 2026 cycle is closed. Confirm the current Computer Science department instructions before applying.
visaConsiderations: Admitted international PhD students complete USC financial and immigration documentation; funding and assistantship terms must be confirmed in the offer rather than assumed from the visa process.
